title: Get Out
logline: A young Black photographer's weekend visit to his white girlfriend's family estate turns into a nightmare when he discovers their seemingly liberal hospitality masks a horrifying conspiracy.
antagonistOrConflict: The Armitage family and their conspiracy to transplant white people's consciousness into Black bodies
tone: Psychological horror with satirical undertones; escalating dread punctuated by moments of dark absurdity
themes: Bodily autonomy and commodification of Black bodies
The loss of identity and agency
Trust and betrayal in interracial relationships

actBreaks.description: Chris enters the sunken place through hypnosis, losing control of his body and agency for the first time.

validation.ruling: Scene #57 (Missy's hypnosis office, p37, ~37%) is a major irreversible turning point: Chris is hypnotized and sent to 'the Sunken Place,' fundamentally changing the story's direction from social awkwardness to psychological horror, raising stakes dramatically, and representing a point of no return that the story cannot revert from.

actBreaks.description: Chris attempts to leave but is trapped by the family, triggering his descent back into the sunken place and full captivity.

validation.ruling: Scene #92 (foyer confrontation, p74, ~74%) is the definitive Act 2 break: Chris attempts to leave but the family blocks him with Dean's bizarre fire monologue, Rose can't find keys, representing total entrapment. This irreversible moment forces Chris into final act survival mode, fundamentally shifting from escape attempt to life-or-death confrontation.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a classic three-act structure but subverts the 'meet the parents' comedy framework into psychological horror. Act One (scenes 1-57, ~37 pages) establishes normalcy then systematically destabilizes it through accumulating microaggressions and uncanny behavior, culminating in the hypnosis scene that literalizes Chris's loss of agency. Act Two (scenes 58-92, ~35 pages) accelerates the paranoia through the garden party's carnival of racist fetishization, Andre/Logan's violent warning, and Rod's comic relief investigation subplot, ending when Chris's escape attempt fails. Act Three (scenes 93-118, ~28 pages) is pure survival horror as Chris discovers the transplant conspiracy and fights his way out. The structure mirrors Chris's psychological journey from discomfort to suspicion to full awareness to desperate action.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The opening kidnapping (scenes 1-13) establishes the horror template before pivoting to Chris's POV. The drive upstate (scenes 14-36) uses extended dialogue and observation to build creeping unease. The script dramatically compresses time once at the estate—Act One covers roughly 24 hours across 57 scenes (0.4 pages per scene average), creating a staccato rhythm of unsettling moments. The garden party sequence (scenes 63-70) is the script's most densely packed section with rapid-fire uncomfortable encounters. The hypnosis scene (scene 57) and sunken place sequences (scenes 58-59, 77, 93) create temporal distortion. Act Three accelerates to breakneck speed with the escape/fight sequence (scenes 110-116) using very short scenes to generate propulsive energy.

incitingIncident.description: Rose refuses to let Officer Crowe see Chris's ID and calls him out for racial profiling until he backs down, then they drive through the woods to the Armitage estate where Dean and Missy greet them warmly.

validation.ruling: Scene #28 (car ride at 11%) is too early and merely transitional. Scene #36 (p17, ~17%) where Chris arrives at the Armitage estate is the true inciting incident: it shatters his ordinary world, establishes the main conflict (unease in this strange household), introduces irreversible stakes (he's now committed to the weekend), and forces him into the central situation he cannot ignore.

midpoint.description: Dean gives a speech comparing the gathering to knights on a crusade as Chris wanders away and meets Jim Hudson, a blind gallery owner who calls the other guests ignorant assholes and offers Chris a solo show.

validation.ruling: Scene #64 (backyard party beginning) is setup, not transformation. Scene #69 (p53, gazebo encounter, ~53%) is the true midpoint: Chris has a revelatory conversation that shifts his understanding, representing the point of no return where suspicions crystallize into action, raising personal stakes and moving him from reactive observation to proactive investigation.

climax.description: Rose chases Chris in her car and shoots him in the arm, they crash and fight hand-to-hand until Chris strangles her as Rod arrives in a TSA car and they drive away together.

validation.ruling: Scene #112 (kitchen at 94%) lacks content for evaluation and seems transitional. Scene #116 (p95-99, sports car escape/confrontation, ~95-99%) is the true climax: highest tension, Chris confronts the ultimate obstacle (Rose's betrayal and the family), demonstrates character arc completion, resolves the main story question of survival, and delivers the final unavoidable confrontation.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - FRONT LAWN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Shaw family eats dinner inside their suburban home at night.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Andre walks alone on a dark suburban street as night falls.

scenes.heading: INT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Shaw family discusses Mickey Mouse over dinner while a biblical quote about presenting bodies as living sacrifice appears on screen.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A vintage cream Porsche with tinted windows follows Andre down the street.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Inside the car, the driver's heavy breathing echoes while a French language tutorial plays through the system.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre tries to communicate with the car following him but receives no response as the vehicle creeps alongside him, stopping when he stops.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver watches Andre through the windshield as the French tutorial continues playing.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre confronts the car and threatens the driver, declaring he's from Brooklyn and ready to fight.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver's point of view shows Andre banging on the passenger window demanding a response.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The car continues to follow Andre in silence.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - FRONT LAWN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Andre's expression turns from anger to terror when the window rolls down to reveal a driver wearing a medieval knight's helmet.
scenes.turningPointNote: First act of violence as Andre is abducted, establishing the horror premise

scenes.heading: INT. THE SHAW’S HOUSE - DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Shaw family continues their meal, oblivious to the violence outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The masked driver carries Andre's limp body to the car and drives away while the Shaw family remains unaware inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN PARK SLOPE - DAWN
scenes.summary: The sun rises over Brooklyn in autumn as the title card "Get Out" appears.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: The camera moves slowly through a small Brooklyn loft apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris shaves in the bathroom, postures in the mirror, and accidentally nicks his neck.

scenes.heading: INT. STARBUCKS COUNTER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Rose looks at pastries through the glass at a Starbucks counter and smiles.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - BATHROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris finishes shaving and cleans up his bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris photographs urban scenes through his window while his dog Sid watches, then the buzzer rings.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN BUILDING - HALLWAY. MORNING
scenes.summary: Chris walks through the hallway of his Brooklyn building.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Rose arrives and greets Chris's dog Sid enthusiastically in the living room.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - CHRIS’ BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose teases Chris about neglecting Sid while Chris packs and tries to smoke, but Rose breaks his cigarette and reminds him to pack a condom.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN LOFT - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris asks Rose if her parents know he's Black, expressing concern about being her first Black boyfriend, but Rose dismisses his worry saying her dad would vote for Obama a third time.
scenes.turningPointNote: Establishes the racial tension that drives the entire narrative

scenes.heading: EXT. BROOKLYN NEIGHBORHOOD STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose blows Sid a kiss as they leave the apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. BROOKLYN NIEGHBORHOOD STREET - PARKED CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris loads his luggage into Rose's messy BMW while she jokes about being sanitary at work, then they take a selfie together.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK - CITY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris drive through New York City together.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - BIRD’S-EYE VIEW - DAY
scenes.summary: The car travels from city to rural road as seen from a bird's-eye view.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose hums while driving as Chris photographs the passing trees and tries to smoke, but Rose throws his cigarette out the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAGUARDIA AIRPORT - OUTSIDE THE TERMINAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris sits in the passenger seat talking to Rod on the phone about LaGuardia Airport security.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris confirms Rod will watch Sid, Rose takes the phone to greet Rod, and Rod warns Chris about white girls taking Black men to secluded locations.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris playfully tickles Rose over a cigarette when a deer suddenly darts in front of the car and smacks the hood before flying into the woods.
scenes.turningPointNote: The deer accident foreshadows Chris's trapped fate and triggers the police encounter

scenes.heading: EXT. UPSTATE NEW YORK ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose recover from the shock and debate whether to check on the deer.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris walks toward the woods and finds the deer gasping and staring at him with a black wet eye while Rose calls the police, and Officer Crowe arrives to investigate.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Officer Crowe questions Chris in a tense exchange about his ID.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S CAR - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Rose sits in the passenger seat deep in thought after the encounter.

scenes.heading: EXT. ARMITAGE ESTATE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Rose refuses to let Officer Crowe see Chris's ID and calls him out for racial profiling until he backs down, then they drive through the woods to the Armitage estate where Dean and Missy greet them warmly.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean gives Chris a tour of the impressive mansion interior with its paintings, taxidermy, and portrait of an old couple above the fireplace while Missy asks Rose and Chris about the drive.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris walk upstairs with their bags despite Dean's offer to have Walter handle them.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE - ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rose and Chris enter her preserved teenage bedroom with pink decor and boy band posters, and Rose tries to initiate sex but Chris insists on taking the house tour first.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DOWNSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean and Chris walk through the house looking at pictures while Dean explains the renovations and shows Chris a closed basement door with alleged black mold issues.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE - HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean jokes about Rose taking after her mother and reveals he's a retired neurosurgeon.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean and Chris enter the pristine kitchen where Georgina, an African American woman, stands facing the wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Dean leads Chris through the huge backyard toward a gazebo while Chris notices Walter preparing the lawn mower in the distance, and Dean begins to explain their Black employees.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTDOOR PATIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Dean awkwardly explains that Walter and Georgina cared for his parents until they died, then he kept them on to avoid putting them out, and he calls Walter over to remove a dead bird from the patio.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of Walter and Georgina's strange behavior establishes the uncanny horror

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DINING ROOM - LATER/NIGHT
scenes.summary: The family sits on the patio drinking iced tea as Chris shares that his mother died when he was eleven after being hit by a car, and Missy presses Chris and Rose about whether they're in love.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE ESTATE. NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rose's brother Jeremy arrives home and pounds on the door demanding to know who answers it.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jeremy energetically tells embarrassing stories about Rose at the dinner table while Dean pours wine and everyone drinks.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: The family continues drinking wine at dinner as Jeremy finishes his story.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Night falls over the estate.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DOWNSTAIRS HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris walks through the dark hallway of the Armitage house at night.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris lies awake after sex with Rose, hears a buzzing and wind, notices the closet door creaking open, then leaves the bedroom to go downstairs where he hears something.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris steps outside into the backyard to smoke a cigarette.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The kitchen light turns on and floods the scene.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris watches in terror as a figure runs across the dark lawn toward him, which turns out to be Walter the groundskeeper jogging past without noticing him.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The scene transitions to the dining room at night.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris encounters Walter running across the yard at night, which terrifies him until Walter emerges from the shadows and passes without noticing.

scenes.heading: INT. MISSY’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Missy brings Chris to her office for hypnotherapy to cure his smoking habit, rhythmically stirring tea with a spoon while asking him questions, then hypnotizes him by having him sink into a memory of his mother's death as he falls through darkness.
scenes.turningPointNote: Missy successfully hypnotizes Chris and sends him to 'The Sunken Place,' revealing her sinister control and marking the shift from social discomfort to supernatural horror.

scenes.heading: INT. DARKNESS
scenes.summary: Chris continues falling through darkness in slow motion as Missy approaches on the screen above him, commanding him to sink as he cries out in terror.

scenes.heading: INT. DARKNESS
scenes.summary: Chris lands at the bottom of a deep well-like space, screams for Mrs. Armitage, and watches helplessly on the screen above as Missy stands over his paralyzed body.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Chris wakes up startled and sweaty in Rose's bed, confused about whether it was a bad dream, receives a text from Rod, and plugs in his low-battery phone.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - DAWN
scenes.summary: Chris walks through the backyard at golden hour with his camera, encounters a nightmarish skeletal deer with glowing blue eyes that charges and impales him on its antlers, then wakes up in Rose's bed realizing it was a dream.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris encounters Walter outside who asks about the hypnosis, triggering Chris's hazy memory of the night before, and Chris realizes the cigarette taste repulses him; he tells Rose about being hypnotized and that it made him nauseous.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - NOON
scenes.summary: Walter directs expensive cars onto the front lawn as wealthy white guests arrive for the party.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - NOON
scenes.summary: The party is in full swing with thirty white guests plus one Japanese man; Chris and Rose mingle and meet Gordon Greene and his wife Emily, who compliment Chris and gush about Tiger Woods.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose meet Nelson Deets in a wheelchair and his trophy wife Lisa, who sizes Chris up with unsettling intensity.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Lisa bluntly asks Rose if the lovemaking is better with a Black man, shocking Chris, then Chris and Rose meet Parker and April Dray who make awkward comments about not caring about skin color.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Parker makes a comment about skin color favor shifting over thousands of years; Chris walks away to the bar where he encounters Andre (Logan), who now speaks in a completely different, formal voice with no urban dialect, creeping Chris out further.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Chris returns to Rose without his drink, visibly more creeped out and agitated.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAZEBO. DAY.
scenes.summary: Dean gives a speech comparing the gathering to knights on a crusade as Chris wanders away and meets Jim Hudson, a blind gallery owner who calls the other guests ignorant assholes and offers Chris a solo show.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - LATER
scenes.summary: Jeremy recruits Chris to play badminton, and Jim tells Chris they could do wonderful things together.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris and Rose play badminton against Jeremy and Derrick in an intense rally.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris wins the badminton point with a diving swat and celebrates while the crowd goes wild, but as he scans the beaming faces, everyone stares at him with unsettling intensity, including Andre who looks particularly glazed over.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris walks to Rose's room to check his phone.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris exits the bathroom and finds his phone unplugged and dead, which strikes him as odd.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris hears a creak and peers into Jeremy's room, seeing Georgina making the bed, then quickly retreats before she notices him.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris plugs his phone in again after finding it mysteriously unplugged and dead.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ APARTMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod watches TV and eats a burger at Chris's apartment.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rose he thinks Georgina is jealous of their relationship, which Rose dismisses as paranoid; Chris calls Rod who warns him about sex slavery and urges him to leave immediately, but Chris hangs up when Rose returns.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rod explicitly warns Chris he's in danger and to leave, marking the moment when Chris's friend articulates the threat, though Chris doesn't fully act on it yet.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Chris apologizes to Georgina for getting her in trouble, and she insists the Armitages treat them like family with a pained smile; Dean then introduces Chris to seven more eager guests who seem to share a private joke.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Chris takes a flash photo of Andre, who suddenly transforms back to his original voice and screams 'GET THE FUCK OUT OF HERE' with blood trickling from his nose before being restrained and carried inside by Phil and Jeremy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Andre/Logan's violent outburst and warning to Chris is a critical moment where the victim breaks through his conditioning to warn Chris, escalating the horror and confirming the danger.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Dean explains Andre had a seizure and is okay, dismissing the incident as epilepsy triggered by the flash, while Phil and Andre/Logan leave together with Missy watching; the silent party guests then gather at the window with sparklers to watch Chris and Rose leave for a walk.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK YARD. SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The party guests gather silently in the backyard while Chris and Rose are away.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Chris walks with Rose by the lakeside and tells her he wants to leave tonight because something doesn't feel right.

scenes.heading: INT. BACKYARD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Dean conducts a silent auction using hand signals with Chris's photo on an easel while party guests bid on him.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that the party guests are literally auctioning Chris off confirms the conspiracy and marks the full exposure of the Armitages' human trafficking operation to the audience.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Rose tells Chris she's late for her period and might be pregnant, and Chris promises never to abandon her like he abandoned his dying mother, convincing him to stay.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The auction intensifies with rapid bidding between three couples.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Jim Hudson wins the auction for Chris with a bid of ten thousand plus two hundred, and Dean bangs his fist to close the sale.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jim Hudson winning the auction for Chris seals Chris's fate and reveals the blind man's sinister interest in acquiring Chris's body for his eyes.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT LAWN - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rose about abandoning his dying mother and promises never to leave Rose; she convinces him they should go home together, and they walk back toward the house as guests depart and Walter watches them.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE BATHROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris washes his hands in the bathroom and looks in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ APARTMENT - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris receives a text from Rod with a picture of witches sacrificing a man, replies he'll be back tomorrow, then sees a photo of Andre/Logan on his phone that Rod sent.

scenes.heading: INT. ROSE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris calls Rod and confirms the photo is Andre but explains he's different now, acting white and gay; Rod warns him he's in a fucked up situation and needs to leave, but the phone dies mid-conversation.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER/LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris packs his bag in a hurry and looks out the window to see Jim Hudson's driver deliver a green envelope to Missy before driving away without Jim, leaving him behind.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris witnesses Jim's chauffeur delivering payment and leaving without Jim, confirming the transaction is complete and Chris is the purchased product, triggering his decision to flee.

scenes.heading: INT. DARKNESS
scenes.summary: Chris announces he's leaving to find the car keys while Jeremy blocks the door twirling his lacrosse stick; Rose claims the keys are in her bag, but when Dean plays classical music and Missy stirs her teacup, Chris falls under hypnosis again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris is re-hypnotized and paralyzed as he tries to escape, revealing Rose is complicit in the conspiracy and marking the complete betrayal and trap closing around him.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRPORT - SECURITY CHECK - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris falls through the abyss to the Sunken Place again, watching helplessly through the screen above as his paralyzed body is lifted and carried downstairs by Dean and Jeremy while Rose coldly says 'it doesn't matter anymore.'

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRPORT - DEPARTURES TERMINAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Rod arrives at the airport departures terminal.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rod smokes and calls Chris in his living room at night.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ KITCHEN - LATER
scenes.summary: Rod opens dog food while calling Chris again but gets voicemail.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Rod places Sid's food bowl down but Sid won't eat and instead stares at the door, sensing something is wrong.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: The scene transitions to the games room later.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LIVING ROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Rod waits anxiously in Chris's living room at dawn with no word from his friend.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris wakes up strapped to a leather chair in a taxidermy-filled room, panicking and shouting for Rose while trying to free himself, then a television turns on showing footage of deer in the woods.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris's captivity is fully realized—the conspiracy's final stage begins with him physically trapped and helpless.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE STATION - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Rod tells Detective Latoya his theory that the Armitage family is abducting and brainwashing Black people as sex slaves, prompting her to mockingly call in other officers to hear his story.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: The police officers laugh at Rod's conspiracy theory while he desperately tries to convince them Chris is in danger.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Chris remains bound in the chair as 'You've Got a Friend' plays on repeat, weakening him psychologically.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRIS’ LOFT - DUSK
scenes.summary: Chris begs his captors to stop playing the song while Rod sits in Chris's loft trying to call him again, knowing he won't answer.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMITAGE HOUSE. DINING ROOM - DUSK
scenes.summary: Rod calls Chris's phone and Rose answers claiming Chris left two days ago after a fight, but Rod catches her in lies and records the conversation on Garage Band, realizing she's involved.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rod confirms Rose's complicity in the conspiracy, shifting from suspicion to certainty and establishing he has no institutional help.

scenes.heading: INT. GAMES ROOM
scenes.summary: Rose's flirtatious facade drops as she hangs up and looks back at her approving family while Chris sings along to the torture song in resignation.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY (TELEVISION)
scenes.summary: Chris is hypnotized by the teacup on screen, wakes up with his head shaved, and Jim Hudson appears on the television to explain the brain transplant procedure that will transfer his consciousness into Chris's body.
scenes.turningPointNote: The full horror of the Coagula procedure is revealed—Chris learns he will be permanently trapped inside his own body while someone else controls it.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATING ROOM.
scenes.summary: Chris blocks his ears with cotton stuffing from the chair's armrest to avoid being hypnotized during the surgery.

scenes.heading: INT. DARK HALLWAY
scenes.summary: Jeremy wheels the unconscious Chris down a dark hallway toward the operating room where the family awaits.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATING ROOM
scenes.summary: Chris removes the cotton from his ears in the operating room where Jim Hudson lies on one bed and Dean stands in ceremonial robes preparing for the transplant surgery.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris escapes his restraints and attacks Jeremy with the stuffing, beating him unconscious in the kitchen.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris breaks free and begins his violent escape, shifting from victim to active combatant.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chris impales Dean through the neck with mounted deer antlers, causing him to fall and start a fire in the operating room that spreads rapidly.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Missy attacks Chris with a knife and her cat, but Chris smashes her head with the teapot before Jeremy bursts from the basement and tackles him.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT YARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris scrapes Jeremy's keys into his eye and repeatedly slams his head into the floor until Jeremy stops moving, then runs outside.

scenes.heading: INT. SPORTS CAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rose chases Chris in her car and shoots him in the arm, they crash and fight hand-to-hand until Chris strangles her as Rod arrives in a TSA car and they drive away together.
scenes.turningPointNote: Chris defeats Rose and escapes with Rod's help, surviving the conspiracy and achieving freedom.

scenes.heading: INT. MAXIMUM SECURITY PRISON. VISITING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris sits in prison across from Rod who desperately tries to get him to remember names from the party to prove the conspiracy, but Chris insists he doesn't remember.
scenes.turningPointNote: The victory is revealed as pyrrhic—Chris is imprisoned for the murders with no way to prove the conspiracy, showing systemic injustice.

scenes.heading: INT. MAXIMUM SECURITY PRISON. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chris tells Rod he's at peace because he stopped the conspiracy, then guards escort him back to his cell whistling 'You've Got a Friend.'
